name: HandlerRegistry

methods.name: andThen
canHandle
handle
handleLocal
methods.documentation:
Attempts payload-first local handling and writes the outcome into the reusable execution frame. Implementations that cannot preserve the lazy input return {@code false}, causing the caller to use the canonical message-based path.

This method is intended for Fluxzero registry implementations. A {@code false} result means “use the regular path”, not “no handler exists”.

@param execution the current local dispatch and destination for its result @return {@code true} if this registry completed handler selection on the payload-first path; {@code false} to use regular message-based handling

methods.name: handleResult
handleResult
noOp
methods.documentation: A no-op registry that does not register or invoke any handlers.
